source_title: Automated Alignment Researchers: Using large language models to scale scalable oversight
cover_caption: PGR (Performance Gap Recovered) progress over cumulative research hours for Automated Alignment Researchers

ai_summary.one_liner: Nine Claude Opus 4.6 instances achieved PGR 0.97 on weak-to-strong supervision, far surpassing human researchers' PGR 0.23
ai_summary.problem: Alignment research cannot keep pace with frontier model advancement; scalable oversight of superhuman models is unsolved
ai_summary.solution: Automated Alignment Researchers: nine Claude instances with sandboxed workspaces, shared forums, and diverse initial prompts conducting independent alignment research
ai_summary.key_result: PGR 0.97 in 5 days (800 hours, ~$18k) vs human PGR 0.23 in 7 days; math generalization PGR 0.94, coding PGR 0.47
limitations: No significant improvement at production scale (Claude Sonnet 4)
Methods may become alien science difficult for humans to verify

figures.caption: PGR (Performance Gap Recovered) progress over cumulative research hours for Automated Alignment Researchers. AARs achieved PGR 0.97 over 5 days (800 cumulative hours) compared to human researchers' PGR 0.23 over 7 days.
figures.caption_ko: Automated Alignment Researchers의 누적 연구 시간에 따른 PGR(Performance Gap Recovered) 진행 추이. 인간 연구자(7일)의 PGR 0.23 대비 AAR(5일, 누적 800시간)은 PGR 0.97을 달성했다.

figures.caption: Generalization performance of the AAR's best method. Strong transfer on math tasks (PGR 0.94) but limited transfer on coding tasks (PGR 0.47).
figures.caption_ko: AAR이 발견한 최선 방법의 일반화 성능. 수학 과제(PGR 0.94)에서는 강한 전이가, 코딩 과제(PGR 0.47)에서는 제한적 전이가 나타났다.
